memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Anthony Trollope
memes.description: British novelist (1815–1882), author of *He Knew He Was Right* (1869) and the Chronicles of Barsetshire series, one of the most prolific and respected writers of the Victorian era

memes.label: Arabella French
memes.description: One of the two French sisters — along with her sister Camilla — who appear as minor comic figures in the novel's social landscape, ultimately marrying Mr. Gibson.

memes.label: Bozzle
memes.description: A private detective hired by Louis Trevelyan to spy on Emily and Colonel Osborne, whose reports fuel Trevelyan's jealousy and accelerate the marital breakdown.

memes.label: Brooke Burgess
memes.description: Heir to Miss Stanbury of Exeter, a young man of thirty who falls genuinely in love with Dorothy Stanbury, providing one of the novel's few successful romantic resolutions.

memes.label: Camilla French
memes.description: The younger of the two French sisters, a comic figure whose romantic designs on Mr. Gibson and involvement in gossip create minor subplots in the novel's social landscape.

memes.label: Caroline Spalding
memes.description: An American woman traveling in Italy who becomes a romantic interest for Charles Glascock and eventually marries him, representing transatlantic cultural themes in the novel's Italian chapters.

memes.label: Charles Glascock
memes.description: An English aristocrat and heir to a title who travels in Italy and eventually marries Caroline Spalding, representing a transatlantic romantic subplot in the later chapters of the novel.

memes.label: Clock House
memes.description: Miss Jemima Stanbury's residence in Exeter, a center of provincial social life

memes.label: Colonel Osborne
memes.description: Elderly family friend whose innocent visits to Emily catalyze Trevelyan's jealousy

memes.label: Colonial Governance
memes.description: The theme of colonial governance in He Knew He Was Right — Sir Rowley's governorship of the Mandarin Islands as background to the novel's domestic drama.

memes.label: Custody
memes.description: The legal doctrine governing which parent had the right to raise a child following marital separation in Victorian England, and its central role in the tragedy of *He Knew He Was Right*.

memes.label: Dorothy Stanbury
memes.description: Sister of Hugh Stanbury, niece of Miss Stanbury, and a central figure in the Nuncombe Putney and Exeter social worlds. Her quiet dignity and romantic choices form a counterpoint to the Trevelyan marital drama.

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: The concept of duty — to family, spouse, and society — as a structuring moral principle in He Knew He Was Right, shaping character choices and defining the boundaries between acceptable and transgressive behavior.

memes.label: The Osborne Visits
memes.description: Colonel Osborne's visits to Emily Trevelyan that catalyze her husband's jealousy

memes.label: Emily Trevelyan
memes.description: Co-protagonist of Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), the wife of Louis Trevelyan whose steadfast refusal to submit to unjust control forms the novel's central conflict and moral argument

memes.label: Exeter
memes.description: The cathedral city in Devonshire that serves as a key setting in Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), most notably as the home of the formidable Miss Stanbury, whose influence radiates throughout the novel's social world

memes.label: Gender Roles
memes.description: The prescribed and constrained social roles available to women in Victorian England, and how Trollope's female characters in *He Knew He Was Right* navigate, resist, and are shaped by these limitations.

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: The Victorian code of honour as depicted in He Knew He Was Right, governing masculine reputation, feminine propriety, and the social consequences of perceived moral transgression.

memes.label: Hugh Stanbury
memes.description: Journalist and moral exemplar, foil to Trevelyan's pathological jealousy

memes.label: Inheritance Law
memes.description: The legal framework governing the transmission of property and title across generations in Victorian England, and its effects on characters' fortunes and decisions in *He Knew He Was Right*.

memes.label: Jealousy
memes.description: The central destructive emotion of Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), explored through Louis Trevelyan's pathological suspicion of his wife Emily, which the novel treats as a form of mental illness rather than moral failing

memes.label: Lady Milborough
memes.description: An elderly London society matron who takes an active interest in the Trevelyan marriage, acting as a mediator, confidante, and vehicle for the novel's exploration of social opinion and its limits.

memes.label: Lady Rowley
memes.description: Wife of Sir Marmaduke Rowley, mother of Emily Trevelyan and Nora Rowley. Her perspective on the Trevelyan marriage — shaped by maternal concern and colonial distance — provides a crucial external view of the novel's central conflict.

memes.label: London
memes.description: Primary metropolitan setting of the novel, center of Trevelyan's social and professional life

memes.label: Louis Trevelyan
memes.description: The central protagonist of Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), a wealthy young gentleman whose pathological jealousy destroys his marriage, his sanity, and ultimately his life

memes.label: Madness
memes.description: The theme of mental deterioration in He Knew He Was Right — Trevelyan's descent from rational jealousy into "insane jealousy," and the Victorian medical and moral frameworks used to understand his condition.

memes.label: Mandarin Islands
memes.description: Fictional colonial territory governed by Sir Rowley, representing Britain's imperial periphery

memes.label: Marcus Stone
memes.description: British illustrator and painter (1840–1921) who provided the original illustrations for Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869)

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: A central thematic concern of Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), examined through the catastrophic breakdown of the Trevelyan union and contrasted with successful partnerships throughout the novel

memes.label: Martha
memes.description: Emily Trevelyan's loyal maid and confidante, who appears prominently in the early chapters of the separation when Emily confides her plans to leave the marital home.

memes.label: Miss Stanbury
memes.description: The wealthy, strong-willed matriarch of the Stanbury family in Exeter. Miss Stanbury's fortune, opinions, and imperious character shape the lives of her relatives and the social world of the cathedral close.

memes.label: Money
memes.description: The role of money, inheritance, and financial independence in He Knew He Was Right — a material substrate beneath the novel's romantic and psychological plots.

memes.label: Mr. Bideawhile
memes.description: The elderly family lawyer to Louis Trevelyan, who attempts to mediate the marital crisis through legal channels and personal counsel. His failure to influence Trevelyan illustrates the limits of professional authority against pathological obsession.

memes.label: Mr. Crump
memes.description: A lawyer and brother of one of the French sisters' associates who appears in the novel's final chapters to facilitate a settlement involving Miss Stanbury and the French sisters.

memes.label: Mr. Gibson
memes.description: A vacillating suitor in Exeter whose protracted seven-year flirtation with Dorothy Stanbury ends in nothing, serving as a foil to the decisive courtship of Brooke Burgess.

memes.label: Mr. Outhouse
memes.description: An East-end London clergyman, married to Emily Trevelyan's aunt, who provides shelter to Emily and her child at St. Diddulph's during the Trevelyan separation.

memes.label: Mrs. Outhouse
memes.description: The wife of Mr. Outhouse and aunt to Emily Trevelyan, who provides practical and emotional support to her niece during the Trevelyan separation at the St. Diddulph's parsonage.

memes.label: Mrs. Stanbury
memes.description: The widowed mother of Hugh, Priscilla, and Dorothy Stanbury, living in reduced circumstances at Nuncombe Putney. Her quiet dignity and practical management of a diminished household embody Trollope's respect for unassuming domestic virtue.

memes.label: Nora and Hugh Courtship
memes.description: The courtship of Nora Rowley and Hugh Stanbury, providing the novel's affirmative romance plot

memes.label: Nora Rowley
memes.description: Emily's sister, whose courtship with Hugh Stanbury provides the novel's affirmative romance plot

memes.label: Nuncombe Putney
memes.description: A rural Devonshire village in Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869) that serves as a place of refuge and exile for the novel's female characters, embodying the contrast between rural innocence and London corruption

memes.label: Obedience
memes.description: The Victorian doctrine of wifely obedience — a husband's right to command and a wife's duty to obey — and its role in the breakdown of the Trevelyan marriage.

memes.label: Personal Freedom
memes.description: The concept of personal freedom in He Knew He Was Right — the struggle for individual autonomy within the constraints of Victorian marriage and social convention.

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: The role of pride — both as dignified self-respect and destructive arrogance — in He Knew He Was Right, where Trevelyan's wounded pride fuels his jealous obsession.

memes.label: Priscilla Stanbury
memes.description: Elder sister of Dorothy and Hugh Stanbury, living at Nuncombe Putney. Known for her sharp tongue, fierce independence, and undisguised hatred for her wealthy aunt Miss Stanbury.

memes.label: Propriety
memes.description: The Victorian code of proper social conduct — and the consequences of its real or perceived violation — as a driving force in the Trevelyan marital crisis.

memes.label: Reconciliation
memes.description: The attempts at marital reconciliation between Louis and Emily Trevelyan, and the forces — pride, jealousy, and social pressure — that repeatedly prevent it.

memes.label: Reputation
memes.description: The Victorian concept of reputation — social credit that must be carefully managed and is easily destroyed — as depicted in He Knew He Was Right.

memes.label: Scandal
memes.description: The social mechanism of scandal in He Knew He Was Right — how private conduct becomes public spectacle and the role of gossip in enforcing Victorian social norms.

memes.label: Sir Marmaduke Rowley
memes.description: Governor of the Mandarin Islands, father of Emily Trevelyan and Nora Rowley. His absence in the colonies and delayed return to England contribute to the isolation of his daughters during the Trevelyan marital crisis.

memes.label: Sir Peter Mancrudy
memes.description: A prominent Exeter legal authority and friend of Miss Stanbury, whose opinions on marriage law and social standing carry significant weight in the novel's provincial society.

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: Social class and hierarchy in He Knew He Was Right — the Victorian class system as a framework shaping character identity, courtship, and professional choice.

memes.label: Social Custom
memes.description: The role of social custom and convention in He Knew He Was Right — the unwritten rules governing Victorian domestic life, courtship, and public reputation.

memes.label: Social Norms
memes.description: The unwritten codes of respectable conduct that governed Victorian social life, and how adherence to or defiance of these norms drives the conflicts of *He Knew He Was Right*.

memes.label: The Daily Record
memes.description: The fictional London newspaper in Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869) where Hugh Stanbury works as a journalist, representing the rising profession of journalism in Victorian society

memes.label: Trevelyan Death
memes.description: Louis Trevelyan's death, the tragic conclusion of his jealous obsession

memes.label: Trevelyan Household
memes.description: The domestic establishment of Louis and Emily Trevelyan in Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), whose dissolution mirrors the collapse of the marriage at its centre

memes.label: Trevelyan Jealousy Crisis
memes.description: The overarching dramatic arc of Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), from Louis Trevelyan's first suspicions through his descent into madness and death, encompassing the novel's exploration of jealousy, marriage, and mental illness

memes.label: The Trevelyan Separation
memes.description: The pivotal event of Anthony Trollope's *He Knew He Was Right* (1869), in which Louis Trevelyan's pathological jealousy forces a separation from his wife Emily, setting in motion the novel's central tragedy

memes.label: Trust
memes.description: The foundational expectation of mutual faith and confidence between spouses in Victorian marriage, and the destructive consequences of its erosion in *He Knew He Was Right*.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The epistemological theme of truth, evidence, and certainty in He Knew He Was Right — Trevelyan's conviction that he "knows" his wife's guilt, and the novel's implicit critique of masculine certainty.

memes.label: Virtue
memes.description: Victorian moral virtue as depicted in He Knew He Was Right — the qualities of character that distinguish admirable from contemptible behaviour in Trollope's moral universe.
